us-politics.headline: Trump Escalates Iran Threats as Military Jets Shot Down
us-politics.body:
The US-Iran conflict sharpened this week as Trump warned Tehran that 'time is running out' before 'all hell' rains down, while Senator Lindsey Graham urged Iran to 'choose wisely.' Trump separately described a deal as 'near completion,' leaving his administration's endgame unclear. Military losses are mounting - US jets shot down by enemy fire mark the first such losses in over 20 years, a significant battlefield development.

At home, the administration arrested two relatives of slain Iranian general Qasem Soleimani after Secretary of State Rubio revoked their green cards. Critics see the move as political signaling; supporters call it a national security necessity. Analysts remain divided on what 'finishing the job' against Iran would actually require, with some arguing the US can prevail through attrition without ground troops.

sources.title: Sunday shows preview: Trump ups threats on Iran after saying war ‘near completion’
TEVI TROY: Trump faces the burdens of a wartime presidency
US military jets hit in Iran war are the first shot down by enemy fire in over 20 years
The US can beat Iran at its own game, and without boots on the ground
ICE arrests relatives of slain Iranian general Soleimani living in US after Rubio revokes their green cards
Rubio revokes green cards of 2 Soleimani relatives amid Iran conflict
Graham backs Trump’s Iran ultimatum as deadline approaches: ‘Choose wisely’
In Iran, what does ‘finish the job’ even mean?
Trump warns Iran: ‘Time is running out’ before ‘all hell’ rains down

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Proposes Defense Surge, Deep Domestic Cuts
us-politics.body:
The White House unveiled a budget blueprint seeking $1.5 trillion in defense spending while proposing sharp reductions across domestic programs. The proposal reflects Trump's stated priority of military expansion, but faces resistance in a Congress where even Republican allies have raised concerns about cuts to programs their constituents rely on.

Former White House press secretary Kayleigh McEnany warned publicly that the 2026 midterms 'will be hard' for Republicans, a rare candid admission from within the party's orbit. A separate Gallup poll showing China now outpacing the US in global approval ratings adds external pressure, suggesting the administration's posture abroad is costing diplomatic standing even as it pursues military strength.

sources.title: McEnany sounds alarm: Midterms ‘will be hard’ for Republicans
Trump unveils $1.5T defense surge, deep domestic cuts — what’s on the budget chopping block
China now tops US in global approval ratings: Gallup
America is heading for a recession — and it may be the worst yet

us-politics.headline: Courts Check Trump on Race, Ballroom and College Policy
us-politics.body:
A federal judge blocked the Trump administration's effort to require colleges to certify they do not consider race in admissions, handing a legal setback to one of its higher education priorities. Separately, Trump filed an emergency appeal to continue construction of a White House ballroom after a court moved to halt the project, with a lawsuit challenging the renovation still unresolved.

The Wisconsin Supreme Court race is drawing national attention as a test of Republican strength in a key swing state, with the state GOP facing an uphill battle. In California, a clash between the FBI and Democratic congressman Eric Swalwell is complicating his gubernatorial bid, while an opinion piece in The Hill argues Trump's pipeline order in California sets a precedent for federal overreach into state authority.

sources.title: Wisconsin judicial race spotlights state GOP’s struggles
What we know about the White House ballroom bunker
Trump files emergency appeal to keep building White House ballroom
Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions
Trump crossed an oily line of state sovereignty in California — your state is next
Swalwell-FBI clash draws attention to Trump foe’s California governor bid

us-politics.headline: ICE Crash Dispute, Louisiana Parade Incident Fuel Immigration Debate
us-politics.body:
The Department of Homeland Security pushed back hard against Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen after he claimed an illegal immigrant caused a crash while fleeing ICE, with DHS disputing key details of his account. The exchange reflects the broader partisan battle over how immigration enforcement is conducted and communicated to the public.

A separate incident in Louisiana, where a vehicle drove into a crowd at a parade injuring several people, drew immediate attention amid a heightened national focus on public safety events. Authorities have not confirmed the circumstances or motive. FBI Director Kash Patel also made headlines with a blunt warning to anyone who attacks law enforcement, saying the bureau would 'put you down' - language that drew both praise from supporters and criticism from civil liberties advocates.

sources.title: DHS slams Democrat Sen Chris Van Hollen claim, says illegal alien caused crash while fleeing ICE
Several injured after vehicle drives into crowd at Louisiana parade
FBI's Patel delivers blunt warning to law enforcement attackers: 'We're going to put you down'

au-politics.headline: Fuel prices bite as NSW targets price-gouging service stations
au-politics.body:
NSW is boosting funding for its FuelCheck app and urging motorists to report service stations suspected of unjustified price hikes, as fuel costs continue to squeeze household budgets. The move follows growing community pressure on governments to act against what critics describe as opportunistic pricing at the bowser.

On the ground, the impact is already reshaping daily life. Some Australians are changing how they commute, and for lower-income households food is becoming, in their own words, a privilege. The NSW government's approach leans on consumer reporting rather than direct price controls, a distinction that critics argue stops well short of meaningful intervention.

sources.title: Motorists urged to dob in service stations over fuel price hikes
Fuel prices force commute changes, as food becomes 'privilege' for some

au-politics.headline: Melbourne murder suspect caught fleeing on international flight
au-politics.body:
A man was arrested at Melbourne Airport on Saturday after allegedly murdering a woman, with police intercepting him as he attempted to board an international flight. Details of the victim and the relationship between the two have not been fully disclosed by authorities.

The arrest raises questions about alert systems at Australian airports and how quickly law enforcement can act when suspects attempt to flee across borders. Police have not yet indicated where the man was attempting to travel.

sources.title: Man arrested attempting to board international flight at Melbourne airport after allegedly murdering woman

au-politics.headline: Gambling reform slammed as Labor's response falls short
au-politics.body:
ACT Senator David Pocock has publicly condemned the federal Labor government's response to gambling reform, arguing it amounts to little more than tinkering at the edges of a serious public health crisis. Writing in the Guardian, Pocock says the government's measures fail the millions of Australians harmed by gambling-related damage each year.

Labor has resisted calls for stricter advertising bans and betting limits, citing industry and broadcaster concerns. Pocock and other advocates argue that incremental reform protects commercial interests over community welfare. The government has not publicly responded to the latest criticism.

sources.title: We cannot celebrate tinkering when it comes to gambling reform. Labor’s response falls tragically short | David Pocock

au-politics.headline: Victoria's First Nations treaty body holds historic first elections
au-politics.body:
Victoria's First Peoples' Assembly is conducting its first elections for the treaty negotiating body, a milestone in what would be Australia's first formal treaty process between a state government and its Indigenous peoples. Candidates and community members have emphasised the importance of self-determination, with one participant quoted saying 'we're the experts in our own lives.'

The process has broad support among First Nations communities in Victoria, though treaty negotiations remain complex and no agreement has yet been reached with the state government. The elections mark a structural step forward regardless of where those talks ultimately land.

sources.title: ‘We’re the experts in our own lives’: Victoria’s First Nations treaty body is holding its first elections

world.headline: US-Israel Strike Iran as Trump Issues 48-Hour Ultimatum
world.body:
The United States and Israel have escalated military strikes on Iranian infrastructure, hitting universities, bridges, and a Tehran psychiatric hospital. A US fighter jet was shot down over Iran, and the search for the missing airman continues, presenting what analysts describe as a serious diplomatic and military test for Washington. Tehran has rejected Trump's ultimatum demanding a deal within 48 hours, with Iranian officials trading threats of 'hell' with their American counterparts.

The US has arrested relatives of slain Iranian general Qasem Soleimani, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio stripping Soleimani's niece of her US residency. A fire broke out at a Kuwait oil complex amid the broader regional tensions. Neighbouring Pakistan faces mounting pressure to navigate the fallout, while Italy's Meloni met the Qatari emir to discuss energy security as markets brace for supply disruption.

sources.title: Iran war live: Tehran rejects Trump’s ultimatum; fire at Kuwait oil complex
US and Iran trade threats to unleash 'hell' as search for missing US airman continues
US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
Search for missing airman presents serious test for US
What we know so far about the US fighter jet shot down over Iran
Tehran psychiatric hospital not usable after US-Israeli strike
Universities hit as US, Israel ramp up attacks on Iran’s infrastructure
Iran’s coastline: architecture of control
Italy’s Meloni meets Qatar emir to discuss energy issues amid Iran war
Marco Rubio says he has stripped Qassem Soleimani’s niece of US residency
New video reveals moment US-Israeli munitions impact Iranian bridge
Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal as search for US pilot continues

world.headline: Israel Tightens Grip on Hebron Mosque, Arrests Antiwar Protesters
world.body:
Israeli authorities have moved to restrict access to the Ibrahimi Mosque in Hebron, deepening Palestinian concerns over the site's status. Israeli police also arrested antiwar demonstrators in Tel Aviv, signalling growing domestic pressure on the government over its military campaigns. Drone strikes continued in central Gaza, with footage showing a vehicle engulfed in flames.

Indonesia received the bodies of UN peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon, highlighting the human cost of the wider regional conflict. Displaced Lebanese civilians describe lives 'turned upside down' as the fallout from ongoing hostilities continues to ripple across the region.

sources.title: Israeli measures tighten grip on Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque
Indonesia receives bodies of peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon
Israeli police arrest antiwar protesters in Tel Aviv
Vehicle engulfed in flames after Israeli drone strike in central Gaza
Displaced in Lebanon: 'Lives turned upside down'

world.headline: Russia Kills Five in Ukraine Market Strike
world.body:
A Russian attack on a market in Ukraine killed five people, the latest in a series of strikes targeting civilian areas. The attack drew renewed condemnation but no immediate shift in the diplomatic stalemate over the war.

Separately, Germany is considering requiring males under 45 to obtain military approval before taking extended stays abroad, a move reflecting Berlin's sharpening focus on defence readiness as European security concerns deepen.

sources.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad

world.headline: Car Hits Louisiana Parade; New York Baby Shooting Suspect Charged
world.body:
A vehicle ploughed into a Lao New Year parade in Louisiana, injuring around 15 people. Authorities are investigating the circumstances of the crash. The incident follows a pattern of vehicle attacks on public gatherings that has prompted renewed scrutiny of crowd safety measures at community events.

In New York, a man has been charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a baby in a pram, a case that drew widespread outrage. The charge marks a significant step in an investigation that shocked the city.

sources.title: Car slams into Louisiana Lao New Year parade, injuring about 15 people
Man charged over fatal shooting of baby in pram in New York

ai.headline: Claude Code Leak Weaponized With Malware by Hackers
ai.body:
Hackers are distributing a leaked version of Anthropic's Claude Code tool bundled with malware, turning a software leak into an active threat vector. The incident puts pressure on Anthropic at an awkward moment - the company separately announced that Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw support, a pricing move likely to frustrate developers already invested in the platform.

Meanwhile, researchers at Anthropic published findings on how emotion concepts function inside large language models, and Andrej Karpathy shared a public "idea file" framed around LLM applications. The AI ecosystem is generating news on multiple fronts simultaneously, from security incidents to pricing friction to foundational research.

sources.title: Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model
LLM Wiki – example of an "idea file"

ai.headline: AI Fakes and Copyright Trolls Are Targeting Folk Musicians
ai.body:
A folk musician named Murphy Campbell has become a case study in how AI-generated fakes and opportunistic copyright trolls can combine to devastate independent artists. Fake versions of Campbell's music circulated online, and rather than receiving protection, Campbell faced legal pressure from a copyright troll exploiting the confusion.

The story lands alongside a broader cultural anxiety about authenticity. The Verge reports that creators are increasingly being asked to prove their work is human-made, with a new "human-made" logo emerging as a kind of certification mark. The demand for proof of human authorship signals how thoroughly AI has eroded baseline trust in creative work.

sources.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
Really, you made this without AI? Prove it

ai.headline: Right-to-Repair and Digital ID Laws Face Industry Pushback
ai.body:
Tech companies are lobbying hard to weaken Colorado's right-to-repair law, one of the strongest in the United States, by pushing amendments that would hollow out its core consumer protections. The effort follows a familiar playbook - accept the headline, gut the substance.

In Europe, Germany's implementation of the eIDAS digital identity standard has drawn criticism for a different reason: the architecture requires users to have an Apple or Google account to function, effectively handing two American platform giants a chokepoint over European digital identity infrastructure. Both stories reflect the same tension between legislative intent and the practical power of large technology companies to shape how laws actually operate.

sources.title: Tech companies are trying to neuter Colorado’s landmark right-to-repair law
German implementation of eIDAS will require an Apple/Google account to function

ai.headline: Linux 7.0 Cuts PostgreSQL Performance in Half on AWS
ai.body:
An AWS engineer has reported that Linux 7.0 cuts PostgreSQL performance by roughly 50 percent, a serious regression for one of the most widely deployed database stacks in cloud infrastructure. The fix is not straightforward, suggesting the performance hit could persist across production environments for some time.

The finding is a reminder that kernel upgrades carry real operational risk, particularly at scale. AWS running PostgreSQL on Linux represents an enormous slice of global database workloads, and a regression of this magnitude will force many teams to delay kernel upgrades or absorb the performance cost.

sources.title: AWS Engineer Reports PostgreSQL Perf Halved by Linux 7.0, Fix May Not Be Easy

btc.headline: Bitcoin Gains Political and Institutional Credibility After Global Shocks
btc.body:
A former UK Chancellor has publicly backed Bitcoin as an alternative to what he called failing financial systems, adding political weight to a growing institutional narrative. Research from Mercado Bitcoin reinforces the case - Bitcoin has historically outperformed both gold and equities in the aftermath of major global shocks, recovering faster and delivering stronger returns.

The timing matters. With macro uncertainty elevated, Bitcoin is attracting endorsements from figures who once represented the traditional financial order. That shift in tone from establishment voices is harder to dismiss than retail enthusiasm.

sources.title: Bitcoin tends to outperform gold and stocks after global shocks, Mercado Bitcoin finds
Ex-UK Chancellor backs bitcoin as alternative to failing systems

btc.headline: Digital Asset Treasuries Face Pressure to Generate Real Returns
btc.body:
Corporate Bitcoin and crypto treasury strategies are under scrutiny. A new opinion piece argues that simply holding digital assets on a balance sheet is no longer enough - treasuries must now demonstrate yield, utility, or strategic value to justify the position.

The critique lands at a moment when several firms have built identities around Bitcoin accumulation. Passive holding worked when prices climbed. In a more volatile, competitive environment, the bar is rising.

sources.title: Digital asset treasuries must now earn their keep

btc.headline: Jimmy Song Pushes for a Conservative Bitcoin Node Client
btc.body:
Bitcoin developer and educator Jimmy Song is calling for a dedicated node client built around conservative principles - one that resists protocol changes and prioritizes stability over new features. His argument targets what he sees as creeping complexity in Bitcoin's software ecosystem.

The proposal reflects a long-running tension in Bitcoin development between those who want the protocol to evolve and those who treat ossification as a feature. Song sits firmly in the latter camp.

sources.title: Jimmy Song explains why Bitcoin needs a 'conservative' node client

btc.headline: 24/7 Stock Trading and Telegram's Iran Ban Both Signal Shifting Power
btc.body:
Two separate stories point to the same underlying theme: legacy gatekeepers losing control. The push toward round-the-clock stock trading would eliminate the after-hours window where thin liquidity has long allowed outsized price moves - a structural advantage that has frustrated retail traders for years.

Meanwhile, Telegram founder Pavel Durov says Iran's government ban on the platform backfired, driving user growth rather than suppressing it. Both cases illustrate how attempts to restrict open systems often accelerate the adoption of alternatives.

sources.title: Telegram founder Pavel Durov says Iranian government's ban backfired
Traders are the big winners as 24/7 stocks will finally end the after-hours price 'manipulation'

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Mushroom Galette
food.description: A free-form pastry tart with caramelised leeks and shiitake mushrooms bound in a white miso béchamel
food.tip: Freeze your butter for 10 minutes before grating it into the flour — it creates flakier layers than cutting it in by hand

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy shopping bags, a clear 10-metre stretch of floor
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer's carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries with deliberate rest periods — brutally effective for grip strength, posture, and core stability in a way most gym sessions never touch

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers like Derek Parfit extended this to human consciousness, asking whether 'you' tomorrow are truly the same person as 'you' today given constant cellular and psychological change.
learn.why: On a quiet Sunday it's a genuinely unsettling and productive rabbit hole — Parfit's book Reasons and Persons will rewire how you think about continuity, memory, and why you bother planning for your future self at all
